description:
AO has a mature skill registry system (ops_skill.rs) with semver resolution, lockfiles, and multi-registry support, but it lacks remote skill discovery and installation. The official MCP Registry API (registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/v0/servers) is now stable and should be the primary integration target.

## Gap Analysis (vs OpenClaw ClawHub)
- ClawHub hosts 13,700+ community skills with semantic search, usage signals, and a lockfile-based install model
- AO's skill system has catalog metadata management but no HTTP-based artifact download or registry fetching
- AO's MCP surface is limited to `ao mcp serve` — no `ao mcp search/install/update` commands

## Scope
1. Integrate the official MCP Registry REST API as a first-class skill source in `SkillRegistrySourceConfig`
2. Add `ao skill search` with keyword + semantic filtering against the MCP Registry
3. Add `ao skill install <server-id>` that fetches metadata from the registry, resolves transport (stdio/SSE/HTTP), and configures the MCP server locally
4. Add `ao skill update` to check installed skills against registry for newer versions
5. Extend the existing lockfile pattern (`SkillLockStateV1`) to pin MCP server versions with integrity hashes
6. Support scoped installation: project-level (`.ao/state/`) and user-level (`~/.ao/`)

## Non-Goals (for now)
- Building our own registry/marketplace (use the official MCP Registry)
- Skill publishing from AO (can be added later)
- Vector/embedding-based search (start with the registry's built-in search API)

## References
- MCP Registry API: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/about
- Existing skill system: crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/operations/ops_skill.rs
- Existing MCP server: crates/llm-mcp-server/
title: MCP Skill Discovery and Installation Layer
